programmes.show_description: Action from the latest matches, including Hull City v Millwall, Ipswich Town v Leicester City and Bristol City v Coventry City in the Championship

programmes.show_name: Long Lost Family: Born Without Trace
programmes.show_description: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present the documentary that combines new DNA technology with painstaking detective work to find answers for foundlings. In 1966, infant Simon was left in the outside toilet block of a mother and baby home in Neath in south Wales. In an unrelated incident, four years later in Christchurch, England, newborn Lisa was left in another baby's pram. Almost 60 years later, Simon and Lisa are still desperate to know who left them and why
Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

AJ Odudu and Vernon Kay host the grand final as the three remaining designers go head-to-head in their biggest challenge yet by creating their own full collections and showcasing their designs in a star-studded catwalk event. They need to impress not only M&S lead designers in this finale, but three fashion experts David Gandy, Jodie Kidd and Marco Capaldo, before one is crowned champion and wins a life-changing chance to work alongside M&S

The presenter visits North Wales, and is joined by fellow culinary expert Stephen Terry. Their first stop is the grand Pale Hall, where head chef Gareth Stevenson is cooking Moroccan-inspired Welsh lamb
James cooks up a feast of delicious food for comedian Alan Davies, enjoys a dish from chef Sami Tamimi, and learns all about beans from gardener David Domoney.

Jimmy Doherty and Shivi Ramoutar are joined in the kitchen by baker and writer Juliet Sear, who enjoys kimchi pancakes for breakfast and shares her tips for carrot cake. There is a lesson in sculpting clay chicken ornaments with crafter Jade Berry, as well as a sangria recipe with a twist
A round-up of the latest headlines

programmes.show_name: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programmes.show_description: Alan is joined by comedian Alan Davies, wildlife presenter Michaela Strachan and Call the Midwife stars Annabelle Apsion and Cliff Parisi. The programme also meets a couple who have spent the past five years setting up a hedgehog hospital in their back garden

It has been more than a year since the fire in the farmhouse caused so much smoke damage that it became uninhabitable, and the day has now finally arrived for a clear-out and renovation work to begin. Elsewhere, some new hens arrive for cockerel Daniel, and Liz and Kelvin enjoy a day off as they visit another farm in Cheshire, unearthing a new passion for homegrown vegetables

Chef Ainsley Harriott celebrates the Bahamas' festival spirit with art and food at the Baha Mar resort. He also has a fritto misto masterclass with chef Scott Conant, and rustles up barbecue jerk-spiced chicken in a sweet guava glaze
A weekly round-up of royal news, with expert analysis from ITV News' royal editor Chris Ship and expert Lizzie Robinson, and guests

James crosses over the Irish Sea to Northern Ireland, where he is joined by famous local chef Paul Rankin. Their first port of call is the Giant's Causeway, where James cooks a fish stew for the crowds, followed by a visit to a family farm in Limavady, where Paul learns how their award-winning rapeseed oil is produced. Things get competitive as the chefs compete in a hovercraft race, and finally James takes to the seas to fish for mackerel
Round-up of the latest headlines

Ant and Dec host as more performers take to the stage at the auditions in Blackpool, trying to impress judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and KSI and secure their place in the live semi-finals

Quiz game show hosted by Lee Mack in which the questions have nothing to do with remembering facts, but are all about logic and common sense. A hundred contestants begin every show, but who will make it to the end and answer a question only one per cent of the country can get right - and win up to £100,000?
Round-up of the latest headlines

Rob Brydon hosts the grand finale of the game show, with the remaining nine contestants facing one last series of high-stakes duels. Two players will compete in a best-of-three final, but only one of them will conquer the Floor and take home the £50,000 jackpot

programmes.show_name: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programmes.show_description: Another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with the four lifelines Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, 50-50 and Ask the Host available to them if they get stuck. Jeremy Clarkson hosts

After headmaster Michael Polly sees his school's first XV rugby team snatch a victory from the jaws of defeat, he arrives home with daughter Alana to find no sign of his wife Sarah, a music teacher at the school. The next day sees an inscrutable Michael report Sarah missing to DS Annie Cassidy, who begins to investigate. As Annie observes the curious headmaster, Sarah's disappearance takes a sinister turn. Psychological thriller, starring Eve Myles and David Morrissey
Round-up of the latest headlines

Hundreds of women in the UK become victims of revenge porn and deepfake imagery every year, but the chance of conviction for these crimes is very low. This edition of the Exposure strand is about three women forced to take matters into their own hands after finding themselves portrayed as offering sexual services in faked profiles, followingtheir five-year hunt for answers and for justice, as the women unravel something far bigger than they could have imagined and the one man at its heart

Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present the story of Sue Stalley, who was a teenager when she became pregnant and whose mother was so ashamed she would not allow her to keep her son. The second case features Melanie Mackney, who was adopted and learnt from her paperwork that her mother had twins before she was born, and the mystery of what happened to them has haunted her entire life

The detectives unravel long-forgotten secrets and have a potential circumstantial case against two suspects, but they need more to prove their case in court - fortunately, they get a breakthrough after an appeal for new witnesses with testimony that changes everything. Can the detectives finally uncover what happened to Carol?
Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

It has been more than a year since the fire in the farmhouse caused so much smoke damage that it became uninhabitable, and the day has now finally arrived for a clear-out and renovation work to begin. Elsewhere, some new hens arrive for cockerel Daniel, and Liz and Kelvin enjoy a day off as they visit another farm in Cheshire, unearthing a new passion for homegrown vegetables
Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features
A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions
The key headlines from the day so far

Stephen Mulhern hosts as contestants Fern Britton, Dominic Brunt and Kelle Bryan 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for the charity of their choice

The reigning champions take on sisters Jo and Lucy and couple Elisha and Joseph, using their powers of deduction to work out words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords. The last remaining duo have the opportunity to win the money they've banked earlier in the show to potentially double their winnings, or leaving with nothing. Adil Ray hosts

Ranvir Singh hosts the quiz as Taylor and Jake, Sia and Elvis, and Mel and Melanie take on Riddlemaster Henry Lewis as they attempt to answer general knowledge questions and confusing conundrums. Only one team will make it through to the high-stakes final with a chance to win a cash prize

Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

Bradley Walsh hosts as David from Rhyl, Jasmine from London, Jiorji from Manchester and Sal from Redhill e work as a team to take on one of the experts in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught
A round-up of the day's news headlines and talking points from the UK and around the world

It is all hands-on deck as a break in the weather offers a chance to save the valuable harvest, while the family witnesses eight piglets being born, but the older ones wreak havoc with the farm's water supply. Liz and Marnie are in the top field checking on the ewes, and Kelvin gets hands-on with some of the feistiest animals on the farm
Sarah sets Cain straight, while Liam urges Cain to inform Moira, who then struggles with the news
Theo uses his birthday to make Todd pay, Jodie resents Adam for lecturing her, Leanne worries about Sam, and Lauren puts a dampener on Ollie and Amy's date

Annie is struck by Michael's strangeness in the wake of an awful discovery, while Alana seeks comfort from her dad, but he appears to be in denial. As the case escalates, Ivan reminds Annie that her duties are restricted to family liaison, while Michael is frustrated by the detective and Becky's constant presence in the house
A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents
Current affairs show tackling the issues behind the headlines from a Scottish perspective, with studio debate and analysis of major stories
